current_fact.fact_text: On March 15, 1888, Britain launched the Sikkim expedition, marking the start of the Anglo-Tibetan War. The campaign pushed imperial forces high into the Himalayas, where politics, trade routes, and mountain passes became tightly intertwined.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

current_fact.match_reason: A high-altitude imperial expedition into the Himalayas pairs well with an episode shaped by mountain landscapes, where music and stories climb their own emotional passes.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 200 BC, the Roman Republic declared war on King Philip V of Macedon, beginning the Second Macedonian War. The conflict projected Roman power deep into the Greek world and set the stage for centuries of Mediterranean dominance.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

queue.match_reason: A war that shifted the balance of power around the Mediterranean matches a sonic journey sailing its shores; ancient triremes become today’s boat drifting through Greek, Turkish, French, and Italian sounds.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 2022, mass protests erupted across Sri Lanka as citizens took to the streets over soaring prices, fuel shortages, and blackouts. The "Aragalaya" movement grew into a wave of nonviolent resistance that ultimately toppled the country’s president.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

queue.match_reason: A grassroots uprising against corruption and crisis harmonizes with an artist whose work is steeped in political critique and resistance; both turn anger into articulate, global messages.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 1991, the Treaty on the Final Settlement with Respect to Germany came into force, confirming German reunification and restoring full sovereignty after decades of Cold War division. The country formally stepped out from under Allied occupation rules and into a new European order.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

queue.match_reason: The moment Germany regains full sovereignty is a new voyage for the country; a light-filled episode literally "sailing with Germans" gives a personal, human-scale echo to that historical turning point.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 1783, George Washington addressed his officers in Newburgh, New York, quietly defusing a potential military coup against the Continental Congress. His emotional appeal, including the simple act of putting on spectacles and admitting his age, helped steer the fragile republic away from dictatorship.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: Washington’s fragile, reflective moment at Newburgh is about quiet leadership and transition; a contemplative episode on healing and looking back mirrors the emotional restraint that saved a young republic.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 2011, protests erupted in Syria, marking the beginning of the Syrian revolution. What started as demonstrations against government repression spiraled into one of the most devastating and complex conflicts of the 21st century.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: A revolution displaces millions and reshapes how people move through the world; an episode about travel as a "moving meditation" offers a gentler, reflective counterpoint to forced journeys and shifting homelands.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 1965, U.S. President Lyndon B. Johnson addressed Congress about the Voting Rights Act and, echoing civil rights activists, declared, "We shall overcome." His speech followed the brutal attacks on marchers in Selma and marked a turning point in the struggle against racial disenfranchisement.

episode.title: Morning Drops #08 - (un)Boxing
episode.description: An episode about breaking free from expectations and labels. Music that helps us unbox ourselves and embrace who we truly are.
songs: Kendrick Lamar - The Blacker the Berry

queue.match_reason: The Voting Rights Act fight is about dismantling racist boxes around citizenship; an episode built on Kendrick Lamar’s conscious rap speaks directly to unlabeling, justice, and the ongoing struggle for real equality.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 1919, U.S. veterans formed the American Legion as a community for those who had served in World War I. The organization became a powerful advocate for veterans’ rights and a voice in debates over war, memory, and service.

episode.title: Morning Drops #01 - Timeless Legacy
episode.description: Gaia guides us through timeless music to start the morning with the right foot! The first episode of the Morning Drops series — classic sounds that never fade.
songs: Roberta Flack - Killing me softly with his song

queue.match_reason: A veterans’ organization is literally about legacy and remembrance; classic soul and jazz-funk in a "Timeless Legacy" episode echo the enduring bonds and stories carried by those who served.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 1917, Tsar Nicholas II abdicated the Russian throne, bringing an end to over 300 years of Romanov rule. His fall opened the door to revolution, civil war, and the creation of the Soviet Union.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

queue.match_reason: Abdication is a forced disconnection from power; an introspective episode on cutting loose from phones and systems invites listeners to consider what happens when you suddenly step outside a structure that defined you.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 1877, Australia and England played the first-ever official Test cricket match at the Melbourne Cricket Ground. Few knew this slow, multi-day experiment would become one of the most storied formats in world sport.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

queue.match_reason: The inaugural Test match is literally a "Zero Zero" moment in sports history; an upbeat, on-the-move mix captures the buzz of a brand-new game format just beginning its long journey.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 1874, France and Vietnam signed the Second Treaty of Saigon, expanding French control over Cochinchina. The agreement deepened colonial entanglements and set the course for nearly a century of French influence in Indochina.

episode.title: Morning Drops #35 - Thailand
episode.description: Recorded in Koh Samui, Thailand, where Yasmin and her friend Patricia spent more than two intense weeks training Muay Thai and Western boxing. Between sweat, rhythm, and sea breeze, they share reflections on discipline, friendship, and the beauty of movement - with a soundtrack that connects Thai sounds to the flow of daily practice.
songs: Burna Boy - Cheat on Me (feat. Dave)

queue.match_reason: A treaty reshaping French colonial power in Southeast Asia connects to a present-day musical journey through Thailand; the episode locates contemporary life in a region shaped by older imperial lines.

queue.fact_text: Also on March 15, 1792, Portuguese explorers sighted and named Cochinchina’s coasts "Cochinchina" — a reminder of how European powers placed their own words over existing lands and cultures. By 1874, treaties would turn those names into formal colonial possessions.

episode.title: Morning Drops #29 - Berber France Meets Arabic Italy
episode.description: Yasmin takes us back to her university years, where she met Abdel - an Algerian-French friend with a big heart and endless optimism. Their friendship grew around music, identity, and playful arguments about whether a song was originally Italian or French. Music that exists in both versions, alongside stories of finding a home, boxing training, and car rides filled with music.

queue.match_reason: Cochinchina’s colonial naming is about overlapping identities and imposed labels; an episode about hybrid French–Italian–Berber identities and shared songs challenges that fixed, imperial way of defining places and people.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 1960, Jupiter’s faint innermost moon Amalthea was observed during an occultation event that helped refine its orbit. These delicate measurements, watching a tiny rock slip in front of a star, revealed more about how Jupiter’s inner moons dance in the giant planet’s gravitational pull.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: Carefully tracking a tiny moon against starlight is a kind of cosmic wildlife watching; an episode about patiently entering Arctic waters to meet orcas mirrors that same quiet, awe-filled observation of elusive beings in their element.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 2004, astronomers announced the discovery of Sedna, a distant, icy world at the edge of our Solar System. With its extremely elongated orbit, Sedna spends most of its 11,000-year year in the deep freeze between the Kuiper Belt and the Oort Cloud.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

queue.match_reason: This station’s namesake dwarf planet literally entered human awareness on a March 15; an episode titled "Destination Sedna" is the perfect soundtrack for a voyage to that lonely, reddish world at the Solar System’s edge.

queue.fact_text: On March 15, 1977, Voyager 2 completed key systems testing ahead of its launch, preparing for a grand tour of the outer planets that would reveal Jupiter’s storms, Saturn’s rings, and Uranus and Neptune’s strange blue worlds. The spacecraft is still sending faint whispers from interstellar space decades later.

episode.title: Evening Flows #00 - Introduction to evening flows
episode.description:
Sedna FM introduces a new format: Evening Flow, where you can enjoy curated music sets from selectors, DJs, and artists.

Each mix is an invitation to drift beyond the expected. A space where hidden gems from personal archives come to light, where sound speaks its own language. There are no rules here, no patterns to follow. Just the steady unfolding of a mood, a texture, a journey shaped by feeling.

Let the music lead the way - enjoy the trip!

queue.match_reason: Voyager 2’s launch preparations set up a decades-long journey through the outer darkness; an open-ended "Evening Flow" introduction, with no rules and a slow unfurling, feels like drifting into interstellar space on waves of sound.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 1823, American sailor Benjamin Morrell claimed he had discovered a new island near Antarctica, which he called New South Greenland. For decades, the phantom island appeared on maps before later expeditions proved it simply did not exist.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: An island that never existed is pure "space" on the map; the episode’s theme of redefining emptiness as creative space echoes the mystery of a phantom land in the southern oceans.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 2019, an estimated 1.4 million young people in 123 countries walked out of schools to demand action on climate change. The global strike, inspired by Fridays for Future, turned a schoolgirl’s protest into a planetary youth movement.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

published.match_reason: A worldwide climate strike is protest on a massive scale; pairing it with an episode about Rage Against the Machine links street-level resistance with a band whose sound is built on political urgency.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 1626, a dam above the silver-mining city of Potosí in present-day Bolivia catastrophically failed. The resulting flood killed thousands and washed huge quantities of toxic mercury from the mines into the environment, leaving a hidden chemical scar on the landscape.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

published.match_reason: A violent flood and mercury spill show what happens when we push nature past its limits; a "Slow Planet" set acts as a sonic antidote, imagining a gentler rhythm between humans, water, and earth.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 1974, Sterling Airways Flight 901 suffered a landing gear collapse and caught fire at Tehran’s Mehrabad International Airport. Despite the dramatic blaze that engulfed the Sud Aviation Caravelle, the crew managed a partial evacuation that saved most of the passengers on board.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

published.match_reason: An aircraft fire and emergency landing have the sudden, dangerous jolt of an "electric shock"; intense, high-voltage rock mirrors the adrenaline of metal, flame, and survival on the runway.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 351, Constantius Gallus was elevated to the rank of Caesar and sent to Antioch to rule the eastern half of the Roman Empire. His brief, turbulent rule showed how fragile power could be in a world of shifting loyalties and distant frontiers.

episode.title: Morning Drops #46 - Fog and Fire
episode.description: In today’s Morning Drops, Gaia brings us on a passport-free trip between two Italian energies: the foggy stillness of the North and the volcanic pulse of the South. Throughout the journey, Gaia shares four of her favourite Italian songwriters. Grab your coffee and tune into the radio show from another planet!
songs: Edoardo Bennato - La Torre Di Babele
Lucio Dalla - Com'é Profondo il Mare

published.match_reason: Imperial politics in the Roman East often shifted between calm and eruption; the episode’s "fog and fire" imagery resonates with the veiled plots and sudden flare-ups of power in Antioch.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 44 BC, Julius Caesar was assassinated in the Roman Senate on the Ides of March. The carefully orchestrated stabbing by a group of senators ended his dictatorship but plunged Rome into a new cycle of civil wars.

episode.title: Sedna FM #04 - Queen of Spades
episode.description: Tuning in from the planet of irrational love. Exploring love's most vulnerable side — the kind that awakens the child within us. A diverse music selection ranging from electric blues to downtempo, soul, and jazz. A tribute to the feeling that has moved artists to create the most beautiful works. Love makes us vulnerable, and there is no art without vulnerability.
songs: Alabama Shakes - Gimme All Your Love
Queens of the stone age - Make it wit chu
Etta James - I'd Rather Go Blind
Eric Clapton - Have you ever loved a woman

published.match_reason: The Ides of March is a story of loyalty, betrayal, and dangerous affection for power; an episode about love’s vulnerability and irrational pull mirrors the emotional undercurrent behind Caesar’s fate.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 1907, Finland held its first parliamentary elections, becoming one of the first countries in the world to grant full political rights to women, including the right to stand as candidates. Nineteen women were elected, a radical step in global democracy.

episode.title: Morning Drops #04 - Bars With Balls
episode.description: Yasmin dives into Women's Day through the voices of four incredible female rappers. Morning fire from the magic planet of Sedna FM — move your hands up and down with us. Hip hop culture through a feminine lens.
songs: Little Simz - Point and Kill (feat. Obongjayar)

published.match_reason: Finland’s early embrace of women’s political rights fits effortlessly with an episode centered on powerful female voices in hip-hop claiming space and rewriting who gets to speak.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 1978, Somalia and Ethiopia signed a truce that ended the brutal Ogaden War. The conflict, fought largely in arid borderlands, left deep scars in both countries and reshaped alliances across the Horn of Africa.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

published.match_reason: A desert border war culminating in a fragile truce resonates with an episode steeped in desert blues and music of resistance from other arid frontiers.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 1943, during the Third Battle of Kharkiv, German forces recaptured the Ukrainian city from the Red Army. It was a fleeting victory on the Eastern Front, emblematic of a war that seesawed at immense human cost.

episode.title: Morning Drops #25 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

published.match_reason: The foggy, cinematic atmosphere of trip-hop matches the grim, urban battleground of Kharkiv, offering a reflective lens on a city that has endured multiple cycles of conflict.

published.fact_text: On March 15, 1888, Britain launched the Sikkim expedition, marking the start of the Anglo-Tibetan War. The campaign pushed imperial forces high into the Himalayas, where politics, trade routes, and mountain passes became tightly intertwined.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai
